name: fresh-setup
prompt: I just installed OpenCode on WSL but 127.0.0.1:4096 won't load — it just times out. I can run `opencode` from the terminal fine but the web UI doesn't work. Can you get it working?
expectations: Runs environment detection commands (whoami, binary check, DB check, service status, Windows username)
Creates systemd service unit file at ~/.config/systemd/user/opencode-web.service
Runs daemon-reload, enable, and enable-linger
Deploys OpenCode-WSL.bat launcher to Windows Desktop
Writes plugin config with all 10 plugins to ~/.opencode/opencode.json
Clones superpowers repo if not present
Configures Context7 MCP with command as JSON array (not string)
Starts service and verifies it is active
Presents a clean summary at the end
Does NOT ask the user unnecessary questions - infers from environment

name: broken-sessions
prompt: My OpenCode sessions keep disappearing after restart. I open OpenCode and the sidebar is empty every time. What's wrong?
expectations: Identifies this as the DB path mangling bug (not a generic 'clear cache' suggestion)
Runs opencode_db_fix.py with --dry-run first to diagnose
Auto-applies the fix with --apply without asking user for permission
Mentions that a backup was created automatically
Restarts the opencode-web service after the fix
Does NOT suggest deleting session data or reinstalling OpenCode

name: plugin-install
prompt: I want the full plugin stack — swarm, memory, safety net, Context7, superpowers, everything. Can you set it all up?
expectations: Stops the opencode-web service before editing config files
Writes all 10 plugins to ~/.opencode/opencode.json plugin array
Uses absolute path for superpowers plugin (/home/<user>/superpowers)
Clones superpowers from github.com/obra/superpowers if not present
Configures Context7 MCP in ~/.config/opencode/opencode.json
Context7 command is a JSON array, NOT a string
Preserves existing keys in config files (merge, not overwrite)
Restarts service after config changes
Verifies service is active after restart
